data.title: Ganesh Infraworld shares list at 90% premium over IPO price on NSE Emerge
data.description:
Ganesh Infraworld shares list at 90% premium over IPO price on NSE Emerge

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Ganesh Infraworld shares listed on the NSE Emerge platform with a premium of 90 percent on December 6 after nearly 370 times subscription to the Rs 98 crore SME issue.

The shares of Ganesh Infraworld were listed at Rs 157.7 per share on the NSE SME, a premium of 90 percent against the issue price of Rs 83.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The price band for the issue was Rs 78 - 83 per share.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

The West Bengal-based company offers construction and allied services for industrial civil projects, residential & commercial buildings, road, railway infrastructure, power and water distribution projects, specialising in engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) services. It has an order book of Rs 574.9 crore as of August 31, comprising 41 ongoing projects.

Ganesh Infraworld intends to spend Rs 70 crore out of the net issue proceeds for its working capital requirements; and the remainder for general corporate purposes. It will use Rs 40 crore in current financial year and Rs 30 crore in next fiscal 2026 for its working capital.

data.title: Rate sensitive stocks mixed: bank, auto stocks rise after RBI slashes CRR by 50 bps; realty stocks sulk
data.description:
RBI revised full year FY25 GDP growth to 6.6 percent from 7.2 percent

Rate-sensitive sectors like banks, autos, and real estate showed a mixed performance on December 6, following the Reserve Bank of India's decision to reduce the cash reserve ratio (CRR) by 50 basis points to 4.5 percent, easing growth-related investor concerns. However, the RBI maintained the repo rate at 6.5 percent, aligning with market expectations, and reaffirmed its "neutral" policy stance.

Nifty Bank and Auto indices jumped 0.3 percent each following the RBI MPC decision, while Nifty Realty index was down by 0.6 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Monetary Policy Committee decided by 4-2 majority to keep interest rates unchanged at 6.5 percent, ever since February 2023. On the other hand, the SDF rate remained steady at 6.5 percent and MSF rate was at 6.75 percent.

Governor Shaktikanta Das, in his address, stated that food inflation is expected to remain elevated during Q3 and some easing is likely from Q4FY25. "High inflation impacts consumers by reducing their disposable income, which in turn affects spending and overall economic dynamics," he added.

On the GDP growth front, the RBI revised full year FY25 growth to 6.6 percent from 7.2 percent. This comes on the back of disappointing second quarter GDP growth that fell to 7-quarter low of 5.4 percent YoY. Meanwhile, Q3FY25 GDP growth was downgraded to 6.8 percent from 7.4 percent, while Q4FY25 growth forecast was cut to 7.2 percent from 7.4 percent.

For the next fiscal year (FY26), Q1 GDP growth was also cut to 6.9 percent from 7.3 percent. Q2FY26 GDP growth has been pegged at 7.3 percent.

Meanwhile, inflation projections have largely been revised to the higher end. For full fiscal year FY25, inflation was upgraded to 4.8 percent from 4.5 percent.

data.title: Newgen Software stock rises 3.5% after Jefferies rolls out positive outlook, sees 15% upside
data.description:
Jefferies retained its 'buy' call on Newgen Software with a price target of Rs 1,500.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Newgen Software rose 3.5 percent after international brokerage Jefferies states that it remains constructive on the company's growth prospects, anticipating a 15 percent upside from Thursday's closing price.

The brokerage retained its 'buy' call on the stock with a price target of Rs 1,500.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 10.17 am, shares of Newgen Software were trading at Rs 1,338.15 on the NSE. With this, the stock is up over 13 percent for the past week.

Jefferies remains confident on the management's strong growth outlook, largely driven by its India and West Asia businesses. The management continues to step up efforts in the US, especially among mid-large tier banks, Jefferies noted.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

Last week, Newgen Software bagged a Rs 32.5 crore order from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to implement and maintain the Regulatory Application Management System (RAMS), slated to be implemented over a period of seven years.

Jefferies believes that this recent purchase order from RBI may further drive up deal from other Indian banks for Newgen. On that account, Jefferies feels continuous order inflows and new client additions will support growth for the company.

In the September quarter, Newgen Software reported a 47 percent on-quarter growth in its consolidated net profit to Rs 70.3 crore, while revenue from operations grew 14.8 percent to Rs 361.2 crore. On a year-on-year basis, net profit also surged 47 percent, while revenue rose 23 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement

data.title: MPC meet: RBI cuts India's GDP growth projection to 6.6%
data.description:
India's GDP growth for Q2 FY25 slowed to 5.4%, marking the lowest growth in two years, down from 8.1% in the same period last year.

The Reserve Bank of India on December 6 cut its GDP growth forecast to 6.6 percent for current fiscal year, from 7.2 percent earlier.

The central bank also revised its estimate for Q3 to 7.4 percent from 6.8 percent earlier and Q4 to 7.4 percent from 7.2 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

For Q1 FY26, the central bank revised its GDP projection to 6.9 percent from the earlier 7.3 percent and to 7.3 percent for the September quarter of FY26.

India's economic growth slowed down in the September quarter of FY25 to a 7-quarter low of 5.4 percent on contraction in mining growth, manufacturing and utility services. The second quarter number has now clouded the outlook for the full year growth estimates, according to experts.

However, cutting rates to boost growth won't be as easy an option. Annual retail inflation quickened to 6.21% in October, breaching the central bank's tolerance band for the first time in more than a year.

In the June quarter, GDP growth fell to 5-quarter low of 6.7 percent.

MPC decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5 percent on December 6.

Suraksha Diagnostic shares listed at a discount against the issue price of Rs 441 per share on December 6.

Integrated diagnostic chain Suraksha Diagnostic Ltd stock started the trade at Rs 438 per share on the NSE, a discount of 0.68 percent from the issue price on the NSE.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company's market valuation stood at Rs 2,281.14 crore.

Suraksha Diagnostic IPO allottees may see modest listing gains on Dec 6; analysts recommend trading strategy

The initial public offer of the company got fully subscribed on the last day of share sale on December 3. The Rs 846-crore initial share sale received bids for 1.70 crore shares against 1.34 crore shares on offer, translating into 1.27 times subscription.

Earlier, the company mobilised Rs 254 crore from anchor investors. The Initial Public Offering (IPO) has a price range of Rs 420-441 apiece.

The Kolkata-headquartered company's IPO consists of an offer for sale (OFS) of 19,189,330 equity shares worth Rs 846.25 crore, at the upper end of the price band, by promoters and investor shareholders, with no fresh issue component.

Since the issue is completely an OFS, the company will not receive any proceeds and all the funds will go to the selling shareholders.

data.title: Angel One shares drop 5% after client acquisition falls for second straight month
data.description:
Angel One shares rose over 30 percent in the last three months.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Angel One fell close to 5 percent on December 6 after the company reported a decline in gross client acquisitions during November, the second month in a row.

Its gross client acquisition fell to 60 lakh in November, down from 70 lakh last month. On a month-on-month basis, the gross client acquisition fell 14.6 percent, while it was down nearly 12 percent on a year-on-year basis.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At 09.46 am, shares of Angel One were trading at Rs 3,106.95 on the NSE.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

Apart from that, total platform orders also fell 23.5 percent month-on-month to 13.09 crore. Average daily orders also dipped 6.5 percent on-month to 72.8 lakh from October’s 77.8 lakh, but rose 35.7 percent on a yearly basis, the company stated in its exchange filing.

It's worth noting that November had only 18 trading days compared to 22 in October and 20 in November last year which would've also influenced Angel One's performance during the month.

The Overall Average Daily Turnover (ADTO) declined by 12 percent month-on-month, with the Futures & Options segment witnessing a 12.5 percent drop.

Angel One's overall client base reached 2.8 crore by November-end, up 2 percent month-on-month and 56 percent year-on-year.

data.description:
Angel One shares fall 4% over tepid November business performance

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Angel One fell 4% on December 6 as the brokerage firm posted a muted business performance in November on sequential basis.

Angel One said, in a regulatory filing, that its gross client acquisition fell to 0.6 million from 0.7 million last month. On a month-on-month basis, the gross client acquisition fell 14.6%, while the figure was lower by 11.9% on a year-on-year basis.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The number of orders on the platform also fell by 23.5% on a sequential basis to 130.96 million. However, on a year-on-year basis, the figure increased by 22.2%.

At 9:25 am on December 6, Angel One shares were trading 4% lower at Rs 3,091 apiece. The market capitalisation of the stock is Rs Rs 27,800 crore. Its 52-week high is Rs 3,896 and 52-week low is Rs 2,025.

Overall Average Daily Turnover (ADTO) fell 12% on an overall basis when compared to November, while for the Futures & Options segment, the ADTO was down by 12.5% from last month. The ADTO for both these metrics increased by 24.1% and 23.3% year-on-year, respectively.

data.title: RITES shares jump nearly 5% on securing Rs 148.25 crore order from IIM Raipur
data.description:
RITES Ltd

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of RITES surged nearly 5 percent in early trade on December 6 after the company secured a significant project from the Indian Institute of Management (IIM) Raipur. The order, valued at Rs 148.25 crore (excluding GST), involves RITES serving as the Project Management Consultant (PMC) for the development of Phase II of the institute's campus in Chhattisgarh.

The scope of the project includes execution, supervision, monitoring, and development, to be completed on a cost-plus basis within a timeline of 23 months.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

By 9:20 AM, the stock had climbed 4 percent to Rs 299. So far this year, RITES has delivered returns of over 18 percent, outperforming the Nifty 50's 13 percent gain during the same period.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

Established in 1974, RITES is a public sector enterprise and engaged in transport consultancy and engineering, known for its diversified services and extensive geographical reach. It serves as the exclusive export arm of Indian Railways for rolling stock overseas, except in Thailand, Malaysia, and Indonesia.

The state-run company posted lower results for the September quarter across all key parameters compared to the same period last year. Net profit declined by 25 percent to Rs 82.5 crore, down from Rs 110.2 crore. Revenue also dropped 7.1 percent year-on-year to Rs 541 crore, compared to Rs 582.4 crore in the previous year.

Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation, and Amortisation (EBITDA) fell 23 percent year-on-year to Rs 106.4 crore, down from Rs 138 crore, while the EBITDA margin narrowed to 19.7 percent from 23.6 percent last year.

Despite the dip in performance, the company secured over 90 orders in Q2, achieving an impressive milestone of one order per day. RITES remains optimistic about sustaining this momentum throughout the year.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

data.title: Ramco Systems shares rise over 7% on Korean partnership for aviation
data.description:
The partnership provides Korean aviation companies with the synergy of HIST’s advisory as well as Ramco’s aviation 6.0 software, to streamline processes, optimize supply chain, and enhance engine maintenance.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Ramco Systems shares are higher by over 7% in early trade on December 6, after the company announced a partnership with Hanjin Group of Korea for the aviation business, offering maintenance software in collaboration with Hanjin Info Systems for Korea-based aviation companies.

The partnership provides Korean aviation companies with the synergy of HIST’s advisory as well as Ramco’s aviation 6.0 software, to streamline processes, optimize supply chain, and enhance engine maintenance. For the first time, Korean Air, the national carrier of South Korea will be deploying Ramco's aviation software.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"We will create new innovations in the IT market by combining Ramco Systems’ MRO solution expertise and Hanjin Information Systems & Telecommunication’s technological capabilities in the aviation and logistics," Bong-Sup Byun, CEO, Hanjin Info Systems said. Manoj Kumar Singh, Chief Customer Officer – Aviation, Aerospace & Defense at Ramco Systems said the partnership will open doors to ground-breaking innovation.

The multi-million dollar deal with Korean Air, signed earlier this year, is one of the largest ever for Ramco Systems. "This strategic engagement will accelerate tech transformation of Korean Air's new engine maintenance facility, which would be Asia's largest engine MRO facility," the company said. The Korean Air order is to the north of $10 million, Ramco had said previously, without specifying the exact size.

"...when someone like Korean Air chooses us, it gives us the extra credibility in the market. So other people who are really looking at it will absolutely look at our product because it has been chosen by someone like Korean Air. So that has made a very big difference," Ramco had said, on the Korean Air deal.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Nifty, Sensex open flat ahead of RBI MPC meet outcome; FMCG, auto stocks gain
data.description:
Bajaj Auto, Trent, ITC, Eicher Motors, and Power Grid were the top Nifty gainers.

Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex kicked off the December 6 trading session on a muted note as investors await cues from the outcome of the RBI MPC meet. Auto and FMCG stocks edged higher to lend support while IT and realty stocks spoiled the party on the Street following gains of five straight days.

Investors and market analysts expect the central bank to cut the cash reserve ratio from the current 4.5 percent to boost liquidity in the system following weaker-than-expected GDP data in the July-September quarter.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At about 9:20 am, the Sensex was down 61.00 points or 0.07 percent at 81,704.86, and the Nifty was down 19.90 points or 0.08 percent at 24,688.50. About 1.748 shares advanced, 857 shares declined, and 131 shares unchanged.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

"Markets are keenly eyeing the RBI's MPC meeting and its guidance. A CRR cut could trigger a further rally," said Ruchit Jain, Vice President of Technical Research at Motilal Oswal. "The return of FIIs as net buyers is another significant positive, and if this trend holds, large-cap stocks could attract more interest." He also noted that even a dovish stance from the RBI would boost market sentiment.

Among sectors, Nifty Auto was the top gainer, rising 0.4 percent as gains in Bajaj Auto, M&M, and Eicher Motors lifted the index. The FMCG index followed suit as gains in ITC, Godrej Properties, Britannia Industries and Varun Beveraged opened higher.

Losers included Nifty IT, which slipped 0.5 percent led by losses in TCS, Infosys, Wipro and Tech Mahindra. Realty stocks also slipped 0.4 percent led by DLF and Godrej Properties. The index has gained nearly 37 percent since the start of the year, comfortably outpacing Nifty's 13.6 percent gain over the same period.

Banks, both state-owned and private, traded flat as investors await the RBI MPC meeting guidance.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The mid and small-cap indices jumped 0.1 and 0.4 percent, marking an impressive 10-day streak of outperformance. "Although the sentiment remains positive, the outperformance trend may not sustain, as FII buying is likely to favour large-cap stocks," Jain added. He also highlighted that DIIs have been net sellers for three consecutive days, which could weigh on mid and small-cap performance compared to frontline indices.

Also read: RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das’ last MPC meet before his term ends may leave the repo as is, cut CRR

Canara Bank shares edged higher percent after receiving approval from the Reserve Bank of India to divest its shareholding in Canara Robeco Asset Management Company and Canara HSBC Life Insurance Company by 13 percent and 14.5 percent, respectively, through an initial public offer (IPO).

Aviation software specialist Ramco Systems rallied 7 percent after it entered into a partnership with Hanjin Information Systems & Telecommunication Co (HIST), a leading ICT services company and a subsidiary of the Hanjin Group, Korea. The partnership will enable aviation organizations headquartered in Korea to digitally transform their M&E and MRO operations.

Read more: What will happen to Sensex, Nifty if RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das doesn't cut repo rate today?

Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers was up 2 percent in morning trade after it signed a contract for the construction and delivery of the second ship from a series of four additional 7,500 DWT multi-purpose vessels in Germany. In September, the company secured an order for the construction and delivery of four additional multi-purpose vessels of 7,500 DWT each.

"After a positive opening, Nifty can find support at 24,500 followed by 24,400 and 24,300. On the higher side, 24,800 can be an immediate resistance, followed by 24,900 and 25,000," Hardik Matalia of Choice Broking, said. "The charts of Bank Nifty indicate that it may get support at 53,400 followed by 53,100 and 52,800. If the index advances further, 53,900 would be the initial key resistance, followed by 54,200 and 54,400," he added.

Bajaj Auto, Trent, ITC, Eicher Motors, and Power Grid were the top Nifty gainers. Losers included TCS, Wipro, L&T, Infosys, and Tech Mahindra.
